creative_brief: Clean, warm layout with a single hero image of a real farm (Bundarra or Rachel's Farm). Muted earth tones, no stock photography. CTA button in brand green, placed after the waitlist paragraph. Minimal design — let the story breathe.
subject: The morning I couldn't answer my daughter's question
preheader: She asked where her chicken came from. I had nothing.
body:
Last March, my daughter picked up a tray of chicken from the fridge and asked me a simple question.

"Mum, where did this chicken live?"

I froze. I had no idea. Not the farm. Not the state. Not even whether it had ever touched grass. I'd bought it two days earlier and the only thing I knew was the price.

That moment broke something in me.

I'd spent years on my own farm in Kempsey, learning what regenerative land actually looks like — soil that holds water, animals that move freely, produce that tastes like it's supposed to. But when I stood in my own kitchen, I realised even I couldn't trace most of what my family ate.

If I couldn't, how could anyone?

That's the gap FarmThru exists to close. Not with vague promises on packaging. With actual transparency — every product traced to a real farm you can look up.

Here's how it works: you order online, then collect from our Brookvale hub. No middlemen. No wholesalers. No cold chain running 2,400km before food hits the shelf.

Instead, your pork comes straight from Bundarra Farm. Your eggs from Farmer Brown in NSW. Your salmon from Collins Wild Caught on the South Coast. Your dairy from Paris Creek Farms in South Australia — carbon-neutral, bio-dynamic.

Every single supplier, named. Every farm, real.

This isn't a niche box for people who already shop at farmers' markets. It's a grocery store — priced for families who do a weekly shop and want to stop guessing what they're feeding their kids.

We're building something that doesn't exist yet in Sydney. And right now, we're inviting people to own a piece of it through our equity crowdfunding campaign.

You can join the waitlist with a fully refundable $5 deposit — just to hold your spot before the campaign opens.

I started FarmThru because I wanted to answer my daughter's question. I think a lot of parents want the same thing.

Join the waitlist and be part of what we're building →

This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice. Full details in the disclosure document on Birchal.

Rachel & the FarmThru team
